summary:
CircRNA’s circular structure lacks free ends and is therefore inherently resistant to degradation by exo-ribonucleases like RNase R. This structural stability makes circRNAs more durable than linear RNA molecules in biological environments.  

Note that this summary does not include any non-clinical or irrelevant details such as molecular weight comparisons or specific tissue types unless directly related to the core mechanism of resistance.

The absence of free ends in circRNAs renders them highly resistant to degradation by RNase R, a ribonuclease specialized for linear RNA strands. Their inherent structural integrity allows circRNAs to persist longer in cellular environments compared to conventional linear RNAs.

The absence of free ends in circRNAs renders them highly resistant to degradation by RNase R, a ribonuclease specialized for linear RNA strands. Their inherent structural integrity allows circRNAs to persist longer in cellular environments compared to conventional linear RNAs.. Answer choice: B
